name: Epic FHIR API
what_it_does: Epic's FHIR R4 API enables agents to read and write patient clinical data (records, schedules, medications, results) from Epic EHR systems using SMART on FHIR OAuth2.
use_cases: Retrieve a patient's active medication list and allergy history for pre-visit intake automation
Pull appointment schedules and care gaps to drive proactive patient outreach workflows
Write structured clinical notes or questionnaire responses back into the patient chart
Query diagnostic report results and trigger downstream care coordination tasks
Access longitudinal patient data across Epic-connected health networks via Care Everywhere
not_for: Organizations without an Epic EHR subscription — requires an active Epic customer relationship
Anonymous or public health data research — all access is patient or provider context bound
High-throughput bulk analytics without using Epic's $export Bulk FHIR endpoint
best_when: Your agent is embedded in a healthcare organization that runs Epic and needs chart-level clinical data with patient or clinician identity context.
avoid_when: The health system uses a non-Epic EHR; use the generic FHIR API spec against that vendor's endpoint instead.

auth.notes: SMART on FHIR for patient and clinician-facing apps; SMART Backend Services (JWT client credentials) for system-to-system agents. Apps must be registered in Epic's App Orchard or directly with the health system.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free sandbox environment at open.epic.com with synthetic patient data
pricing.paid_tiers: Production access requires Epic customer agreement; no direct per-call pricing — bundled with Epic EHR licensing
pricing.notes: App Orchard listing and review process required for third-party apps connecting to Epic production environments.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by Epic site; typical limits are 100-500 req/min per app credential; Bulk FHIR export has separate async job limits

requirements.min_contract: Epic EHR customer agreement required for production

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OperationOutcome errors are generally well-formed but Epic often returns 403s for missing scopes without indicating which scope is needed.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GET operations idempotent; POST for resource creation supports conditional create via If-None-Exist header. Flowsheet writes are not idempotent without external dedup.
known_agent_gotchas: App Orchard review process takes weeks — agents cannot deploy to new Epic sites without per-site approval workflows
Each Epic installation may have different FHIR capability statements; always fetch /metadata before assuming resource support
SMART Backend Services tokens expire in 5 minutes — agents must implement token refresh without caching stale credentials
Epic's sandbox uses synthetic data that does not replicate all production edge cases (e.g., merged patient records, proxy access)
Bulk FHIR ($export) jobs are asynchronous and may take hours for large populations — agents must poll status endpoints and handle partial failures

security_score_components.security_notes: SMART on FHIR scopes allow fine-grained PHI access control. Epic enforces app-level and user-level consent. HIPAA BAA required with each Epic customer organization.
